hero.title: Local AI All-in-One Workbench
hero.subtitle:
AI Assistant, Multi-Agent orchestration, Investment Management, Knowledge Base, SOP Engine — all in one.
No server needed. ~10 MB install. Data stays local and secure.

features.title: Modular Architecture, Mix & Match
features.subtitle: Investment Management, Knowledge Base, SOP — three modules that work standalone or together.
features.row1_desc: Real-time dashboard showing deal funnel, pipeline distribution and recent activity. Kanban board with six stages from sourcing to closing, supporting drag-and-drop stage transitions.
features.row2_desc: Manage portfolio and tracked company profiles with industry classification and search filters. Link contacts to companies with role tags; centralized document archiving and retrieval.
features.row3_desc: Multi-dimensional deal scoring for quantified decisions. Customizable SOP templates with execution tracking. Audit-compliant logs, scheduled tasks and heartbeat rules for post-investment monitoring.

ai.title: AI Assistant + Multi-Agent Collaboration
ai.subtitle: Claw Assistant, Prospect-Research scientist cluster, ClawSwarm — three AI engines from single chat to multi-agent orchestration.
ai.assistant_title: Claw Assistant — Built-in AI Chat Engine
ai.assistant_desc: Streaming responses, Markdown rendering, multi-session management. Switch between models for industry analysis, Q&A, code assistance, system diagnostics and more.
ai.fleet_desc: Multi-instance AI Agent management with AI-powered creation and configuration. Model pool supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Deepseek and more. Real-time chat to verify agent status.
ai.evo_desc: Evolutionary-reasoning AI scientist system that auto-decomposes complex tasks and coordinates multiple Scientists. 12 built-in templates covering competitive analysis, code review, market sizing, tech evaluation and more.
ai.swarm_desc: Parallel multi-agent task orchestration — input a goal, AI auto-decomposes it into a DAG dependency graph, agents execute in parallel waves. 5 templates (deep research, due diligence, brainstorm, doc review, competitor monitoring) ready to use.

tutorials.title: Two Paths to Get Started
tutorials.subtitle: Each step tells you what to do, where to do it, and what success looks like. Download and begin.
tutorials.invest_desc: Follow this path to run through the full lead-to-approval flow.
tutorials.invest_s1_desc: Populate the dashboard with sample tasks and records so you can explore the full deal pipeline.
tutorials.invest_s2_desc: Enter a project name, industry and owner into the deal pool to get your first lead into the system.
tutorials.invest_s3_desc: Promote a promising project to a Deal and experience stage progression and to-do tracking.
tutorials.invest_s4_desc: Upload review materials to the document library so Deals and SOPs work from the same source.
tutorials.invest_s5_desc: Once materials are ready, push to approval and let the system handle reminders and decision logging.
tutorials.agent_desc: Five steps from freshly installed to your first working Agent.
tutorials.agent_s1_desc: Verify Node.js, OpenClaw CLI and config files are in place before proceeding.
tutorials.agent_s2_desc: Add at least one primary model so your Agent has reasoning capability.
tutorials.agent_s3_desc: Spin up your first Agent with its own identity, model and workspace.
tutorials.agent_s4_title: Feed Skills or Import a Persona
tutorials.agent_s4_desc: Confirm available Skills, or import a persona config from the AI Assistant page to personalize your Agent.
tutorials.agent_s5_desc: Send your first message in live chat and confirm the Agent responds correctly.

tech.title: Professional-Grade Tech Stack
tech.subtitle: Rust backend for security, Vanilla JS for speed, SQLite for privacy, OpenClaw for intelligence.
tech.tauri_desc: Rust backend + system WebView. ~10 MB installer, no bloated Electron runtime. Cross-platform: macOS, Windows, Linux.
tech.vite_desc: Zero framework dependencies, ultra-lightweight. Millisecond HMR with Vite, native CSS variable-driven theming.
tech.sqlite_desc: Data stays on your machine, secure and compliant. No server deployment needed — install and go. Meets financial-grade data compliance.
tech.openclaw_desc: In-house Agent framework supporting OpenAI, Anthropic, Deepseek and more. 4 operation modes, 8 built-in tools.

download.title: Download ProspectClaw Now
download.subtitle: ~10 MB installer, no extra runtime needed. Install and start working.

docs.title: Three Guides, Pick What You Need
docs.subtitle: No tech background? Read the first one. Some experience? The second. Want to feed an AI? The third.
docs.beginner_desc: No coding needed. Follow 8 steps, 30 minutes from download to chatting. Built-in AI assistant helps if you get stuck.
docs.usage_desc: 6 steps to a fully working setup, plus troubleshooting checklist, daily ops tips, and a 9-concept glossary. For users with some tech background.
docs.agent_desc: Copy the full text and feed it to any AI. Product architecture, troubleshooting decision trees, and full FAQ — lets Claw Assistant or ChatGPT answer ProspectClaw questions.

beginner.title: Start from Zero, We'll Walk You Through
beginner.subtitle: No coding needed. No command line required. Follow these 8 steps and you'll have ProspectClaw running in 30 minutes.
beginner.qb1_text: None. If you can install apps, you're good.
beginner.qb2_text: About 30 min, depends on internet speed.
beginner.qb3_text: Ask the built-in AI assistant, or send a screenshot to ChatGPT.
beginner.editions_subtitle: Same core, different default homepages. You can switch anytime without reinstalling.
beginner.v128_desc: Added SkillHub — an in-app skill store so you can install Agent skills without command line. Chat is also more stable; no more silent message drops.
beginner.steps_subtitle: After each step, check the "How to know it worked" section before moving on.
beginner.s1_p1: Mac or Windows, as long as it's connected to the internet. Phones and tablets won't work — it has to be a computer.
beginner.s1_p2: You don't need to know programming or what a "command line" is. If we need you to type a command later, we'll spell it out word by word.
beginner.s1_p3: Having a tech-savvy colleague helps, but it's not required. The app has a built-in AI assistant that can help.
beginner.s1_check: You have a computer with internet access. That's it.
beginner.s2_title: Just Like Installing Any Other App
beginner.s2_p1: Go to the download page and grab the installer.
beginner.s2_mac_p1: Download the .dmg file, double-click it, drag the icon into the Applications folder. Done.
beginner.s2_mac_p2: The first time you open it, macOS will probably warn you about an "unverified developer" or say it's "damaged". It's not a virus, don't worry. Fix: Apple menu → System Settings → Privacy & Security → find the ProspectClaw block → click "Open Anyway".
beginner.s2_win_p1: Download the .exe file, double-click, click "Next" all the way through. If there's a security warning, choose "Run anyway".
beginner.s2_check: You can find ProspectClaw on your desktop or app list, and it opens when you double-click.
beginner.s3_p1: The first launch will take you to the "Initial Setup" page. It automatically checks if your computer has everything it needs.
beginner.s3_p2: A green check means it's fine. A red X means something's missing — don't worry, follow the instructions below.
beginner.s3_nodejs_p: Node.js is a tool — you don't need to know what it does, just install it. Open your browser, go to "nodejs.org", download and install. Then come back and refresh.
beginner.s3_openclaw_p: Just click the "Install" button on the page and wait a few minutes. It'll install itself.
beginner.s3_terminal_p: It's a text window where you type commands. On Mac, press Command+Space, search "Terminal" and hit Enter. On Windows, press the Win key, search "cmd" and hit Enter. But normally you won't need it — the buttons on the page do the job.
beginner.s3_check: All check items are green. Got a red item you can't fix? Skip it for now, set up the AI assistant in Step 4, and let it help you.
beginner.s4_meta: Set Up Claw Assistant (Your AI Helper)
beginner.s4_p1: Find "Claw Assistant" in the left menu and click it. This is the app's built-in AI assistant — you can ask it anything when you get stuck later.
beginner.s4_p2: But it can't work yet — you need to give it an AI model (like installing a brain for it).
beginner.s4_step1: Click the "Settings" button in the top right
beginner.s4_step2: Fill in the AI model info. You'll need an API Key (basically a password)
beginner.s4_step3: Not sure what to use? Try MiniMax (has free credits), or ask a tech colleague for an API Key
beginner.s4_step4: After filling in, click "Test Connection" — if it says success, you're done
beginner.s4_check: Type "hello" in Claw Assistant and it replies. From now on, ask it whenever you have questions.
beginner.s4_callout_desc: From this point on, whenever you're stuck, just describe your problem in Claw Assistant. It's your personal tech support — no waiting in line.
beginner.s5_title: Fire Up the Main Engine Too
beginner.s5_p1: What you just configured was the assistant's own model. Now you need to set up the main system engine so live chat, Agents and other features can work.
beginner.s5_step1: Find "Model Configuration" in the left menu, add an AI model (can be the same one as Claw Assistant)
beginner.s5_step3: Then go to the "Dashboard" or "Service Management" page
beginner.s5_step5: Wait until it turns green and shows "Running"
beginner.s5_check: Model test passed, Gateway shows green "Running". Won't start? Ask Claw Assistant.
beginner.s6_p1: Find "Live Chat" in the left menu, click in, and type anything — like "Hello, tell me about yourself".
beginner.s6_p2: Got a reply? Congrats, the system is up and running!
beginner.s6_p3: No reply? Check if Gateway is still "Running". If it's green but not responding, tell Claw Assistant "Live chat sent a message but got no reply" and it'll investigate.
beginner.s6_check: Sent a message and got a reply. Core features are working.
beginner.s7_title: It's Working — See What Else Is in Here
beginner.s7_p1: The basics are running. Check these out as you wish — no need to try everything:
beginner.s7_ai1: SkillHub — where you install new Agent skills, find it in the Skills page
beginner.s7_ai2: Prospect-Research Scientist Cluster — multiple AIs collaborate on complex tasks, 12 ready-made templates
beginner.s7_ai3: ClawSwarm — give a goal, AI breaks it into tasks and agents work in parallel
beginner.s7_invest1: Investment Dashboard — bird's eye view of your deal pool
beginner.s7_invest2: Deal Pipeline — kanban board from sourcing to closing
beginner.s7_invest3: Company DB + Contacts — manage companies and people
beginner.s7_channel_p: Feishu/Lark: Setup is complex, consider getting help from a tech colleague. Telegram: Simplest option, takes just a few minutes.
beginner.s7_check: You have a general idea of what's in the system and tried a few things that interested you.
beginner.s8_m1: Ask Claw Assistant first. Just describe your problem, like "Gateway is still red, what do I do?" — it usually has the answer.
beginner.s8_m2: Ask an external AI. Send a screenshot or this web page link to ChatGPT / Claude and say "I'm using ProspectClaw and ran into this issue".
beginner.s8_m3: Check the Advanced Guide. The Advanced Guide has detailed manuals and troubleshooting checklists.
beginner.s8_tip: One more thing: don't reinstall at the first sign of trouble. Most issues have simple fixes. Reinstalling might actually lose your configuration. Ask AI first.
beginner.s8_check: You know who to ask when something goes wrong.
beginner.faq1_desc: That's macOS security, not actual damage. Go to System Settings → Privacy & Security → find ProspectClaw → click "Open Anyway". See Step 2 for details.
beginner.faq2_title: Opened the app and see a bunch of red
beginner.faq2_desc: Red means something's missing. Follow the on-screen instructions to install. If stuck, tell Claw Assistant "XX is red in initial setup" and it'll guide you.
beginner.faq3_title: Clicked Start but Gateway is still red
beginner.faq3_desc: Most likely the model isn't configured properly, or the port is occupied by another program. Tell Claw Assistant "Gateway won't start" and let it diagnose.
beginner.faq4_title: Sent a message but nobody replied
beginner.faq4_desc: Check the Dashboard — is Gateway showing green "Running"? If not, start it first. If it's green and still no reply, ask Claw Assistant to investigate.
beginner.final_title: Done? Check Out the Advanced Guide
beginner.final_desc: The beginner guide got you up and running. To learn how each feature works in detail, check the advanced guide.

usage.title: ProspectClaw Advanced Usage Guide
usage.subtitle:
You probably know what a command line is and have used plenty of software — just not ProspectClaw yet.
6 steps to get the system running, plus daily ops tips, troubleshooting checklist and glossary.
Total beginner? See the Beginner Guide. Need structured docs for AI? See Agent Knowledge Base.
usage.qb2_text: Full setup from install to Agent going live
usage.steps_subtitle: Ordered by real usage flow. Each step explains what, why, and how to know you're done.
usage.order_title: Install → Claw Assistant → Models & Gateway → Agent + Skills → Live Chat Test → Connect Channels
usage.order_desc: Why configure Claw Assistant first? Because if anything goes wrong later, you can just ask it. Like finding the medic before heading into battle.
usage.outline_title: These 6 Steps Will Get You Through
usage.outline2: Set up Claw Assistant as your troubleshooter
usage.outline4: Create an Agent and attach Skills
usage.outline5: Verify the full chain via live chat
usage.s1_title: After Installing, Run Through Initial Setup
usage.s1_p1: After installation, the first launch takes you to "Initial Setup". Don't skip it — check every item. These are the foundation for everything else.
usage.s1_b1: macOS blocked on first open: drag to Applications, System Settings → Privacy & Security → Open Anyway.
usage.s1_b2: Windows OpenClaw install error: check admin permissions, npm cache, and Git HTTPS config.
usage.s1_b3: Linux without desktop: use Web deployment — panel on :1420, Gateway on :18789.
usage.s1_check: Initial Setup shows Node.js, Git, OpenClaw, and config file all passed.
usage.s1_desktop_p: Mac / Windows users: download, install, first launch goes straight to Initial Setup.
usage.s1_web_p: Linux / server scenarios. Default :1420 panel, :18789 Gateway.
usage.s2_title: Get Something That Can Help You Debug
usage.s2_p1: Claw Assistant is not the same as the OpenClaw Agent you'll build later. It's a built-in standalone AI assistant for troubleshooting, Q&A, and log analysis.
usage.s2_b1: Go to the "Claw Assistant" page and configure its model.
usage.s2_b2: Even before your Agent is ready, you already have a helper that can read errors and suggest fixes.
usage.s2_b3: Model issues, Gateway crashes, missing Skill dependencies — just toss them to the assistant.
usage.s2_check: Claw Assistant can chat normally. Your "tech support" is on duty.
usage.tools_desc: Common tasks don't need lengthy descriptions — just tap a card. Not sure which? Start with "Check System Environment" and "One-Click Diagnostics".
usage.tool1_desc: Reads openclaw.json and checks providers, Gateway settings, and common config mistakes.
usage.tool1_tip: When to use: just installed, just changed config, suspect something's wrong
usage.tool2_desc: Checks process, ports and logs to determine if Gateway isn't started, isn't listening, or is in a bad state.
usage.tool2_tip: When to use: chat won't connect, port errors, Gateway acting up
usage.tool3_desc: Checks Node.js, npm, OpenClaw CLI and port status to confirm the base environment is intact.
usage.tool3_tip: When to use: install stuck, things broke after upgrade
usage.tool4_desc: Runs a full checkup across config, models, Gateway, WebSocket, and Node.js.
usage.tool4_tip: When to use: something suddenly stopped working, don't know where to start
usage.tool5_desc: Searches logs for ERROR, WARN, fail keywords to narrow down the problem.
usage.tool5_tip: When to use: you see errors but can't understand them
usage.tool6_desc: Shows which Skills work, which are missing dependencies, and guides you to fix them.
usage.tool6_tip: When to use: Agent can chat but won't use tools
usage.s3_title: Models Control the Brains, Gateway Is the Main Switch
usage.s3_p1: "Model Configuration" sets which AI model OpenClaw uses. "Service Management" controls whether Gateway is running. Both need to be working for the system to come alive.
usage.s3_b1: Configure at least 1 primary model. Better to add 1-2 backups so if the main one goes down, there's a fallback.
usage.s3_b2: Save after model test passes. If you changed config, you may need to restart Gateway for changes to take effect.
usage.s3_b3: Go to "Service Management" and confirm Gateway shows green Running. If not, check the logs.
usage.s3_callout_desc: Some users experienced Gateway flicker before. v1.2.8 improved several areas: less frequent refresh, auto-retry on connection drops, extra delay before restart to avoid conflicts. Upgrading to 1.2.8 significantly reduces these issues.
usage.s3_check: Primary model test passed, Gateway shows green Running.
usage.s4_title: Start Simple — Don't Overload It Right Away
usage.s4_p1: Go to "Agent Management" and create your first Agent. Give it a clear name, define its role, pick a model, and get it running first.
usage.s4_b1: First Agent should do just one thing. Don't stuff all capabilities into it.
usage.s4_b2: Agent ID: all lowercase, short and memorable. Display name: whatever you like.
usage.s4_b3: Model controls how smart it is, Skills control what it can do, identity files control who it acts like.
usage.s4_callout_desc: Skills page now has a "Store" tab. Browse skills online, click to install — no command line needed. Different Agents can have separate Skills directories.
usage.s4_check: Your Agent list has one Agent you created, with at least one key Skill showing as available.
usage.s4_pitfall_p: "I can see Skills on the page so they must be working" — not necessarily. Check if the status shows "Available".
usage.s4_advice_p: Make sure 1-2 core capabilities actually work before adding more.
usage.s5_title: Send a Message — See If It Actually Responds
usage.s5_p1: Go to "Live Chat" and send a message. Everything you configured might "look fine" on their own pages, but only a response here proves the whole chain works.
usage.s5_p2: A response means: Gateway is running, model is callable, Agent loaded successfully, permissions aren't blocking.
usage.s5_callout_desc: You can now see what the Agent is doing — thinking, planning, waiting for approval. A timer shows at the bottom after sending, and if there's no response after 3 minutes, you'll get a timeout notification. No more silent message drops.
usage.s5_test_msg: Hello, in three sentences tell me who you are, what you can do, and which model you're currently using.
usage.s5_check: Got a reply from the Agent. Officially upgraded from "installed" to "working".
usage.s6_title: Want Your Agent on Feishu or Telegram?
usage.s6_p1: The "Messaging Channels" page manages all external connections. Regardless of platform, two prerequisites are the same: Gateway must be running, and you need to publish/approve on the platform side after saving.
usage.s6_b1: Feishu / Lark: Create a custom enterprise app on Feishu Open Platform, enable bot capability, get App ID and App Secret. Recommend WebSocket for event subscription. After saving, you still need to publish the app version on Feishu.
usage.s6_b2: Telegram: Create a Bot via @BotFather, get the Token, prepare allowed user IDs, fill in and save. Simplest external channel.
usage.s6_b3: Saved but not working? Check if Gateway is running, if the platform app is published, and if the bot has been added to the group.
usage.s6_check: The bot can receive and reply to messages on Feishu or Telegram.
usage.s6_feishu_p: Needs App ID and App Secret. Supports built-in and official plugin modes. Bot visible to others only after app version is published.
usage.s6_tg_p: Needs Bot Token and user IDs. Lightest option, best for quickly getting your first external channel working.
usage.habit_desc: After each step, if you're not sure you're done, just ask Claw Assistant: "I just configured models, what should I do next?" Way faster than searching around the UI.
usage.daily_title: Daily Usage: Keep an Eye on These 4 Things
usage.daily_subtitle: Once you're comfortable, what saves time isn't "knowing how to configure" but "knowing where to look".
usage.daily1_desc: Agent suddenly not responding? 9 times out of 10, the model dropped. Check API Key, Base URL, primary model connectivity. If you have a backup model, see if it can take over.
usage.daily2_desc: Personal use? Set to "localhost only". Sharing? Configure Token or password. Don't change security settings without reason.
usage.daily3_desc: Name and role clearly defined, backup before major changes, one Agent per task type. When something breaks, you'll immediately know which one to fix.
usage.daily4_title: Check Logs Before Asking for Help
usage.daily4_desc: Don't rush to reinstall. First check live chat behavior, then logs, then run diagnostics. Only escalate to Claw Assistant when you've truly hit a wall.
usage.daily_pages_desc: Claw Assistant: ask questions, troubleshoot. Service Management: start/stop Gateway, backup config. Log Viewer: Gateway and error logs. Memory Files: Agent work memory. About: check current version.
usage.fix_subtitle: Most common issues, organized by symptom.
usage.fix1_b1: Is live chat connected to Gateway?
usage.fix1_b2: Does the primary model pass the test in Model Configuration?
usage.fix1_b3: Is the Agent bound to a model?
usage.fix1_b4: Is Gateway auth blocking the request?
usage.fix1_b5: Send the specific error to Claw Assistant and let it determine which layer is failing.
usage.fix2_title: Can Chat but Won't Use Skills
usage.fix2_b1: Is the corresponding Skill showing "Available" on the Skills page?
usage.fix2_b2: Missing command-line tools or environment variables?
usage.fix2_b3: Are Gateway tool permissions set too strict?
usage.fix2_b4: Do Agent rules lock out certain capabilities?
usage.fix2_b5: Screenshot the status and send to Claw Assistant — it can usually tell you exactly what's missing.
usage.fix3_b1: Did you recently switch OpenClaw versions?
usage.fix3_b2: Is the current version higher than the recommended stable version?
usage.fix3_b3: Did you recently change providers, tokens, or security settings?
usage.fix3_b4: Figure out what changed first, then fix.
usage.fix3_b5: Screenshot or copy logs to Claw Assistant.
usage.fix4_b1: Don't delete the Agent or reinstall.
usage.fix4_b2: Think about three things: what it could do before, what's wrong now, what you changed recently.
usage.fix4_b3: Toss those three answers to Claw Assistant.
usage.fix4_b4: Check in this order: environment → models → Gateway → Skills → the Agent itself.
usage.rescue_msg: My OpenClaw Agent in ProspectClaw used to reply normally but now it doesn't. Please check each layer — environment, models, Gateway, Skills, Agent config — and tell me the most likely cause and which page to check first.
usage.glossary_subtitle: After reading these, you won't get lost in the jargon.
usage.g1_desc: ProspectClaw is the app interface you see (the cockpit). OpenClaw is the engine running in the background. Without OpenClaw, ProspectClaw is just an empty shell.
usage.g2_desc: Think of it as the central dispatcher. Chat messages, model calls, Agent actions — everything goes through it. If Gateway's not running, nothing works.
usage.g3_desc: An Agent is a "character". It has its own name, model, rules, memory, and skill scope. Creating an Agent = creating a new role.
usage.g4_desc: A plugin that determines what an Agent can do besides chatting. Some Skills require additional command-line tools or environment variables.
usage.g5_desc: Skill store added in v1.2.8. Previously you had to use command line to install Skills; now just search and click install in the UI.
usage.g6_desc: Claw Assistant is a built-in troubleshooting tool. Agents are the AI characters you create and train. One is tech support, the other is your employee.
usage.g7_desc: One model works fine — until it doesn't. AI services occasionally have outages. With a backup, when the primary goes down, it auto-switches and your Agent keeps working.
usage.g8_desc: An Agent's "desk". Identity files, behavior rules, memory files, task materials — all stored here. Think of it as the employee's folder.
usage.g9_title: Should You Chase the Latest Version
usage.g9_desc: No. Stick with the recommended stable version. Upgrading too aggressively might introduce compatibility issues that take more time to fix than they save.
usage.final_title: All Set — Time to Get to Work
usage.final_desc: The full chain is running. Need structured docs for AI? Check the Agent Knowledge Base. Want to explore more features? Head back to the homepage.

agent.title: ProspectClaw Agent Knowledge Base
agent.subtitle: This document is for AIs to read. Copy the full text below and feed it to Claw Assistant, ChatGPT, Claude, or any AI platform that supports knowledge bases — it'll then be able to answer questions about ProspectClaw.
agent.howto_title: How to Use This Knowledge Base
agent.howto_desc: Copy all the content above and paste it into whatever AI you're using. Could be Claw Assistant's built-in knowledge, ChatGPT's custom instructions, Claude's project knowledge, or any platform that supports knowledge base imports. Recommend re-copying after version updates.
agent.final_title: Copied? Go Feed It to Your AI
agent.final_desc: Paste it into Claw Assistant, ChatGPT, Claude, or any AI platform. Want to set things up yourself? Check the beginner or advanced guide.
